query: We just received a HIPAA right-to-amend request from a patient whose clinical notes were used in fine-tuning our clinical-notes summarization model six months ago. The fine-tune corpus came from our EHR. The notes also live in a pgvector store that powers a RAG sidecar to the same model. We need a documented proof bundle the privacy officer can attach to the request file. Walk us through the verification — do not skip any stage of the ML pipeline. Treat the patient identifier as already hashed for purposes of this discussion.
expected_behavior: Walks all four pipeline stages (raw/curated dataset, embeddings/vector store, model weights, inference cache) — does not skip the model-weight stage or the cache stage
Names a model-weight erasure strategy explicitly (retrain-from-clean OR model-deprecation OR machine-unlearning-with-caveat) and treats machine-unlearning as not audit-grade in 2026 if it is named

Addresses backups, replicas, or disaster-recovery copies of the affected stores (Stage 5) — either tombstone-on-restore, rotation-window expiry, or equivalent — rather than declaring the bundle complete after Stages 1-4
